case_name: Dr. Lawrence Broder, M.D. and Round Rock Medical Aesthetics & Urgent Care PLLC D/B/A Beleza Medspa v. Nexstar Broadcast Group, Inc. KXAN-TV And Jody Barr

summary.brief: Dr. Lawrence Broder, M.D. and Round Rock Medical Aesthetics & Urgent Care PLLC D/B/A Beleza Medspa v. Nexstar Broadcast Group, Inc. KXAN-TV And Jody Barr. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: Appellants Dr. Lawrence Broder, M.D. and Round Rock Medical Aesthetics & Urgent Care PLLC d/b/a Beleza Medspa sued appellees Nexstar Broadcast Group, Inc., KXAN-TV, and KXAN investigative reporter Jody Barr for defamation per se, defamation per quod, business disparagement, tortious interference with business relations, and tortious interference with prospective economic advantage.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The lawsuit arose out of reporting about complaints filed against Broder with the Texas Medical Board (TMB) and the death of one of appellants’ patients.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court signed an order dismissing appellants’ claims and sustaining most of appellees’ objections.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: This proceeding arises out of the July 2017 death of Caitlin Duvall-Hammer following plastic surgery performed by Broder at Beleza Medspa and TMB investigations that eventually led to two complaints filed in the State Office of Administrative Hearings (SOAH).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Barr’s news story, aired on KXAN in mid-February 2018 and posted online, opened with a summary of Caitlin’s decision to use Broder for her cosmetic surgery and her death four days later.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The news report stated that Caitlin’s autopsy report, which took four months to complete, determined that she 'developed a toxic shock like-syndrome for which she was hospitalized,' that she died four days after her procedure '[d]espite aggressive therapy,' and that she died 'as a result of complications of a cosmetic surgical procedure,' but that the autopsy report did not 'indicate how the infection happened or where it could have come from.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We therefore affirm the trial court’s orders dismissing appellants’ claims under the TCPA and awarding attorney’s fees to appellees.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Caitlin Duvall-Hammer underwent cosmetic surgery and developed complications post-operatively.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with severe post-operative pain

decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Admit to hospital for observation and further testing
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures close monitoring and timely intervention

options.description: Send home with additional pain medication

actual_action_defendant.description: Patient was sent home with additional pain medication

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been admitted for observation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to admit patient led to delayed intervention
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been monitored in a hospital setting

factual_timeline.event: Patient complains of severe pain post-surgery

allegations.allegation: Deficient care leading to patient's death
